purpose: Agent-free provisioned-environment measurements for candidate screening. Establishes what the reproducible_within_budget rule costs in practice and which toolchains are reachable on the fixed Linux host.

host.note: No uv, pyenv, conda, docker or podman available; all toolchains were provisioned into user-writable paths without elevated privileges.

bucket_definition.basis: Defined by evaluator wall time because paired-pilot-manifest-v1 constrains time and declares no disk or memory field. The medium ceiling continues the evaluator.timeout_seconds = 120 that Phase 2a preregistered and used.
bucket_definition.measurement_condition: cold; no build cache or scratch reuse, matching a fresh paired checkout

toolchains.note: The candidate's suite declares no install and no pyrewire needed; absence only skips one policy-predicate assertion.
NuGet cache size after one build.

toolchains.sha256_verified_against: not verified; publisher ships a detached PGP signature rather than a checksum list
toolchains.note: Largest environment measured. Six SwiftPM dependencies pinned in Package.resolved.

negative_control_failures: CJK keyword did not match across a particle

candidates.note: Built with -p:EnableWindowsTargeting=true. A passing build is not a negative control.
Twice proposed for resource exclusion before measurement; both proposals were wrong.

evaluator_constraints_found: An upstream test moved outside the repository loses repo-local context and then fails for unintended reasons. Two distinct forms were observed: a shell suite deriving the repository root from dirname $0/.., and a pytest suite depending on the repository's asyncio_mode setting.

Using the default-branch version of a test as the evaluator contaminates the negative control with assertions for changes made after the candidate's pull request. The evaluator must be reconstructed at the pull request's point in history.
Both constraints require a small adaptation to the protected evaluator, and that adaptation must be reviewed to confirm it changes no assertion.
known_limitations: Measurements come from one host and one network; provisioning times will vary.
The Swift toolchain archive was verified only by recording its SHA-256, not by checking a publisher checksum list, because the publisher distributes a detached PGP signature instead.
Only two candidates have negative and positive controls; the other two have a passing build or suite, which is weaker evidence.
Environments were provisioned under /tmp and are ephemeral; the identities recorded here are what makes them reconstructible.
